title: 26/1/j/2: Minor rounding discrepancy in test expected values
description:
Two test cases have minor rounding discrepancies (< $1):

1. Single, $250k (2024):
   - Computed: 57874.75
   - Expected: 57875

2. Joint, $500k (2024):
   - Computed: 115749.50
   - Expected: 115750

These are within acceptable tolerance for tax calculations (IRS rounds to whole dollars), but the expected values should document whether they represent:
- Pre-rounding exact calculation
- IRS-rounded values
- Some other convention

Consider adding a comment explaining rounding convention.

title: Encode Medicaid/CHIP eligibility rules (42 USC 1396)
description: MAGI-based Medicaid eligibility and CHIP for children

title: 26/1: Missing subsection (g) - Kiddie tax
description:
26 USC 1(g) - Certain unearned income of children taxed as if parent's income

Per index.rac: status: deferred (parent-child linkage needed)

Key provisions:
- (g)(1): General rule - child's unearned income taxed at parent's marginal rate
- (g)(2): Applicable children (under 19, or under 24 if student)
- (g)(3): Allocable parental tax calculation
- (g)(4): Net unearned income definition ($2,600 threshold for 2024)
- (g)(7): Election for parent to report on own return

Requires:
- Parent-child relationship modeling
- Family unit aggregation
- Cross-return data sharing

title: Encode 26 USC 1(h) - Capital Gains Preferential Rates
description:
Fully encode 26 USC 1(h) capital gains preferential rates.

Current state: Stub file exists at statute/26/1/h.rac with:
- adjusted_net_capital_gain variable (basic formula)
- capital_gains_tax variable (returns 0)
- ordinary_income_tax variable (returns 0)

Need to encode:
- (h)(1)(A)-(F): Full tiered rate calculation
- 0%/15%/20% rate breakpoints by filing status
- 25% rate on unrecaptured section 1250 gain
- 28% rate on collectibles gain
- Integration with 1(j)(2) ordinary income rates

Complexity: High - requires coordinated imports from 1222 (net capital gain), careful tier ordering

title: 26/1: Missing subsection (e) - Estates and trusts
description:
26 USC 1(e) covers tax rates for estates and trusts with compressed brackets.

Per index.rac: status: entity_not_supported

The statute specifies 4 brackets (10%, 24%, 35%, 37%) with thresholds indexed for inflation.

This is deferred pending Trust/Estate entity support in the RAC runtime.

2024 thresholds per Rev. Proc. 2023-34:
- 10%: $0 to $3,100
- 24%: $3,100 to $11,150
- 35%: $11,150 to $15,200
- 37%: Over $15,200

title: 26/1: Unresolved imports in parent file - capital gains references broken
description:
CRITICAL: 26/1.rac imports variables that do not exist:

```yaml
variable income_tax:
  imports:
    - 26/1/h#capital_gains_tax         # FILE DOES NOT EXIST
    - 26/1/h#adjusted_net_capital_gain # FILE DOES NOT EXIST
    - 26/1/h#ordinary_income_tax       # FILE DOES NOT EXIST
```

The formula assumes these variables exist and uses them:
```python
if adjusted_net_capital_gain > 0:
  return ordinary_income_tax + capital_gains_tax
```

Options:
1. Create stub 26/1/h.rac with status: partial
2. Remove capital gains logic from 26/1.rac until (h) is implemented
3. Add conditional logic to handle missing variables

Priority: P1 - Code will fail at runtime

title: Encode ACA premium tax credit (26 USC 36B)
test_runner.py doesn't support syntax: python formulas
26/1: Missing capital gains subsection (h) encoding
description:
Section 26 USC 1(h) - Maximum capital gains rate - is referenced in 26/1.rac but not implemented.

The parent file imports from 26/1/h#capital_gains_tax, 26/1/h#adjusted_net_capital_gain, and 26/1/h#ordinary_income_tax but these files do not exist.

Subsection (h) is complex with 11 subparagraphs covering:
- (h)(1): Rate calculation formula with 0%/15%/20%/25%/28% brackets
- (h)(3): Adjusted net capital gain definition
- (h)(4): 28-percent rate gain (collectibles)
- (h)(5): Collectibles gain and loss
- (h)(6): Unrecaptured section 1250 gain
- (h)(11): Qualified dividend income treatment

Priority: P2 - Important for complete income tax calculation

title: 26/1/j/2: Filing status enum inconsistency
description:
There's an inconsistency between filing status enum values in different files:

**In 26/1.rac (FilingStatus enum):**
```yaml
enum FilingStatus:
  values:
    - SINGLE
    - JOINT
    - SEPARATE
    - HEAD_OF_HOUSEHOLD
    - SURVIVING_SPOUSE
```

**In 26/1/j/2.rac (brackets parameter):**
```yaml
thresholds:
  single: [...]
  joint: [...]
  separate: [...]
  head_of_household: [...]
  surviving_spouse: [...]
```

**In 26/1/j/2.rac (test cases):**
```yaml
filing_status: single    # lowercase
filing_status: joint     # lowercase
filing_status: head_of_household  # lowercase
filing_status: separate  # lowercase
```

The enum uses UPPER_CASE but parameters and tests use lower_case. This could cause issues with threshold_by lookup.

Need to verify:
1. How marginal_agg() handles enum-to-key mapping
2. Whether case sensitivity matters
3. Standardize on one convention

26/1/j/2: Add 2023 bracket verification tests
description:
The j/2.rac file has 2023 bracket parameters but no 2023 test cases.

Current tests:
- 11 tests for 2024
- 1 test for 2018 (single $50k)

Should add tests for 2023 to verify:
1. Parameter values are correct
2. Historical calculation continuity

Example 2023 test case to add:
```yaml
- name: 'Single, $50k (2023)'
  period: 2023-01
  inputs:
    taxable_income: 50000
    filing_status: single
  expect: 6307  # Verify this value
```
